text: When you write code, bugs are almost certain to appear. Identifying bugs is the first step. You might notice your program crashes or gives unexpected results. A systematic approach helps you solve problems efficiently. Instead of guessing, check one part of your code at a time. Many debugging tools can assist you. For example, a debugger lets you pause the program and inspect variables. Another useful tool is logging, where you print messages to track what your code is doing. By looking at these logs, you can see where values change incorrectly. With practice, debugging becomes a logical process, not a frustrating one.
